text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy town, there was a bridge. People liked crossing it—it connected two sides. But every evening, a strange owl named Oliver would wait patiently on its railing.

Oliver wasn't just any owl. Legend had it he used to be a professor of ornithology, but fled a boring lecture. Now, every midnight, he would glide along, keeping watch over dreams.

One autumn night, a little girl named Lily wandered over. She didn't know why; perhaps it was because her backpack felt too heavy. Oliver swooped down and took her under his wing.

"Where are you going, sweetie?" he asked.

"To visit a wise old tree."

"Oh? How interesting! I'm the wise oldest owl here."

That night, Oliver told her all about dreams. He showed her how to chase away the scary dream animals and how to finally see her deep, secret wish—something so silly she didn't even realize it was hers until then.

L
